data.content: This is an **Android tombstone** crash log from a .NET MAUI app using the Mono runtime (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`). Let me identify the key details and attempt symbolication.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i binutils 2>/dev/null | head -5

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using ELF Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
# Try symstore-style URL
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try _.debug variant
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
arguments.description: Try downloading symbols from MS symbol server

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try downloading symbols from MS symbol server

data.reasoningText:
This is an Android tombstone/crash log (not an Apple .ips crash log). The apple-crash-symbolication skill is specifically for Apple platforms (.ips files), so it won't help here.

Let me analyze what we have:

1. **Platform**: Android (arm64)
2. **App**: `com.companyname.MauiTest` - a .NET MAUI app
3. **Signal**: SIGABRT with abort message "not compatible"
4. **Runtime**: Mono (`libmonosgen-2.0.so`)
5. **Build ID**: `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`


6. **Crash location**: The .NET runtime frames (#01-#07) are all in `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, with frame #08 pointing to JIT-compiled managed code in anonymous memory

To symbolicate this Android crash, I need to locate the debug symbols for that specific Mono build and use tools like `addr2line` or `llvm-symbolizer` to map the addresses back to source code.

I'm considering whether Microsoft's symbol server would have the ELF symbols for this Android runtime build, since they typically serve Windows PDBs and macOS dSYMs more readily than Linux/Android binaries. I need to complete the URL I was constructing and then try multiple approaches to get the debug symbols—checking the Microsoft symbol server with the Build ID format, and seeing if addr2line is available to resolve the crash addresses.

arguments.command: which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; dpkg -l | grep -i binutils 2>/dev/null | head -5

# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using ELF Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
# Try symstore-style URL
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try _.debug variant
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
arguments.description: Try downloading symbols from MS symbol server

toolCalls.args: {"command": "which addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; echo \"---\"; dpkg -l | grep -i binutils 2>/dev/null | head -5", "description": "Check available symbolication tools", "initial_wait": 10}

toolCalls.name: bash

BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"

echo ""
# Try _.debug variant
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1", "description": "Try downloading symbols from MS symbol server", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.command:
# Try downloading debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server using ELF Build ID
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
# Try symstore-style URL
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try _.debug variant

toolArgs.description: Try downloading symbols from MS symbol server

data.content: Both the library and debug symbols are available on Microsoft's symbol server. Let me download them and symbolicate.

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-9788e54a9f424daa8fe7ac6679e3c9bc
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
mkdir -p symbols

# Download the library itself
curl -sSL -o symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Download debug symbols
curl -sSL -o symbols/_.debug \
  "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug"

# Verify downloads
file symbols/libmonosgen-2.0.so symbols/_.debug
echo "---"
ls -lh symbols/

data.content: Debug symbols downloaded and verified (Build IDs match). Now let me symbolicate all the .NET runtime frames:

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-9788e54a9f424daa8fe7ac6679e3c9bc/symbols

# Place debug file where addr2line can find it (alongside the .so as a .debug file)
cp _.debug libmonosgen-2.0.so.debug 2>/dev/null

# Symbolicate all libmonosgen frames using the cross-arch addr2line
# Addresses from frames #01 through #07
echo "=== Symbolicating .NET Runtime (Mono) frames ==="
echo ""
for addr in 0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04; do
  result=$(aarch64-linux-gnu-addr2line -C -f -e _.debug "$addr" 2>&1)
  func=$(echo "$result" | head -1)
  loc=$(echo "$result" | tail -1)
  printf "  pc %-12s  %s\n                    %s\n\n" "$addr" "$func" "$loc"
done

data.reasoningText:

- #02 pc 0x2241ac
- #03 pc 0x1abc80
- #04 pc 0x1abd14
- #05 pc 0x16dfa4
- #06 pc 0x16db6c
- #07 pc 0x16de04

arguments.command:
cd /tmp/sv-9788e54a9f424daa8fe7ac6679e3c9bc/symbols

# Symbolicate all libmonosgen frames
echo "=== Symbolicating .NET Runtime (Mono) frames ==="
echo ""
ADDRS="0x222098 0x2241ac 0x1abc80 0x1abd14 0x16dfa4 0x16db6c 0x16de04"
FRAMES=(01 02 03 04 05 06 07)
i=0
for addr in $ADDRS; do
  result=$(aarch64-linux-gnu-addr2line -C -f -e _.debug "$addr" 2>&1)
  func=$(echo "$result" | head -1)
  loc=$(echo "$result" | tail -1)
  echo "  #${FRAMES[$i]} pc ${addr}"
  echo "      Function: ${func}"
  echo "      Location: ${loc}"
  echo ""
  i=$((i+1))
done

  echo "      Location: ${loc}"
  echo ""
  i=$((i+1))
done
